title: cordia koemariae
intro: Cordia koemariae, also known as the Pangi plant, is a species of flowering plant in the bignonia family, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which contribute to its potential health benefits. Some of the health benefits associated with Cordia koemariae include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and aid in the treatment of digestive problems. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through the extraction of its leaves and bark, which can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for various herbal preparations.
